div1.entryFree:
Continent, adj. 1) restraining: have a c. forbearance till the speed of his rage goes slower, Lr. I, 2, 182. -- 2) free from concupiscence: my past life hath been as c. as chaste, Wint. III, 2, 35.
In the following passages it may be taken in either sense: contrary to thy established proclaimed edict and c. canon, LLL I, 1, 262. my desire all c. impediments would o'erbear, Mcb. IV, 3, 64.
